cards.back: Electronic waste (e-waste) refers to discarded electronic devices, including computers, smartphones, televisions, and other electronic equipment.
In 2022, an estimated 50% of plastic waste ended up in oceans, threatening marine life and ecosystems.
The Basel Convention, the Electronic Waste Regulation, and the WEEE Directive are key frameworks guiding e-waste management worldwide.
The UN Environment Programme recommended a 50% reduction in e-waste by 2030 and called for increased circular economy practices.
In 2022, an estimated 74 million metric tons of e-waste were generated worldwide, with the majority ending up in landfills or incinerators.
